obligations.description: Store crossing before shrine tithe is treated as illegitimate winter-taking.

taboos.boundary: Crossing with stores before public offering damages trust.

sacred_places.label: River shrine
sacred_places.access_note: Reached through the same ice window as the store crossing.

calendar_events.activates: Tithe and hauling compete for the same safe ice.

obligations.description: Tax grain disputes must be appealed before measurement closes.

taboos.boundary: Using appeal delay to hide grain marks the miller as false.

sacred_places.label: Mill measure
sacred_places.access_note: Public hearing point beside the mill.

calendar_events.activates: Appeal can suspend grain measurement while crows still feed.

obligations.description: Protected burial material may be used only after public consent.

taboos.boundary: Private use of ancestor ash makes orchard recovery a desecration.

sacred_places.label: Ancestor kiln
sacred_places.access_note: Material source for proposed orchard renewal.

calendar_events.activates: Food recovery and protected material handling are decided together.

obligations.description: First milk must be offered before sale, slaughter, or trade substitution.

taboos.boundary: Trading first milk for salt before offering breaks herd legitimacy.

sacred_places.label: Lambing pen threshold
sacred_places.access_note: Where offering and herd care meet.

calendar_events.activates: Salt caravan and first-milk obligation collide.

obligations.description: Mine work stops when warning signs are rung at the bell post.

taboos.boundary: Ignoring the bell repeats the old collapse betrayal.

sacred_places.label: Mine bell post
sacred_places.access_note: Warning point at the shallow ore pit.

calendar_events.activates: Oath stoppage competes with ore and fuel needs.

obligations.description: The fish feast must be public even when nets are short.

taboos.boundary: Hidden rationing preserves food but violates feast trust.

sacred_places.label: Feast hall weir
sacred_places.access_note: Fish source and public meal site are socially linked.

calendar_events.activates: Weir repair and public distribution collide.

obligations.description: Emergency cutting of grave grass requires public restitution.

taboos.boundary: Private cutting is treated as grave neglect even if fire is near.

sacred_places.label: Widow Hill graves
sacred_places.access_note: Protected grass lies on the fastest firebreak line.

calendar_events.activates: Emergency exception and grave protection are judged together.

obligations.description: Canceling saint-day travel requires public explanation and substitute rite.

taboos.boundary: Closing the gate without substitute rite looks like selfish market control.

sacred_places.label: Saint-day market gate
sacred_places.access_note: Pilgrims, trade, fodder, and quarantine all meet here.

obligations.description: Burners and beekeepers must both contribute to winter peace.

taboos.boundary: Taking fuel or honey without visible reciprocal contribution breaks the bargain.

sacred_places.label: Coppice bargain grove
sacred_places.access_note: Fuel cutting and bee forage overlap.

calendar_events.activates: Coppice, charcoal, honey, and social peace are settled together.

obligations.description: Ledgers may be moved or copied only with witnesses.

taboos.boundary: Quick salvage without witnesses preserves records but makes claims suspect.

sacred_places.label: Flooded record house
sacred_places.access_note: Records, witnesses, and flood routes collide.

calendar_events.activates: Witness legitimacy competes with water timing.
